mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.2pp monthly unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly but SPX 20-day returns remain negative in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals a sudden supply-side contraction or demand shock, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances following a monthly INDPRO decline exceeding 0.3%.
